pattern: letting the model grade its own claim, implementation, or outcome
why: semantic confidence is not deterministic evidence and can manufacture apparent merge readiness
instead: keep GPT-5.6 proposal-only and route every verdict through GateResult

pattern: duplicating canonical facts in AGENTS.md, skills, README, and prose plans
why: copies drift and agents cannot tell which version owns the decision
instead: update the single JSONL authority and keep other surfaces as references or generated views

pattern: using a local file dependency or copying upstream GraphReFly source
why: the project becomes irreproducible outside the author's workspace
instead: consume a pinned npm version or immutable Git commit

pattern: expanding providers, runners, scenarios, or automation before the flagship slice is judgeable
why: breadth weakens implementation depth, design coherence, and evidence before the deadline
instead: advance only the ready STACK phase and leave deferred adapters in backlog

pattern: making UI or README claims without exact witnesses and deterministic gate evidence
why: the product becomes a trust-based model summary instead of a proof surface
instead: link each material validity or invalidation claim to reproducible evidence and reason codes

pattern: running separate generic adversarial review and project QA workflows
why: overlapping reviews duplicate effort and can disagree on whether the milestone passed
instead: use stack-qa as the single combined review and evidence gate

pattern: using blueprint hash alone as a freshness witness
why: semantic implementation or policy can change without a topology hash change
instead: bind commit SHA, blueprint hash, semantic parent, policy revision, source scopes, and required checks

pattern: treating a clean rebase or passing text-level tests as semantic validity
why: non-overlapping changes can invalidate the architecture assumptions behind a work unit
instead: recompute GateResult against current blueprint and dependency witnesses

pattern: presenting a GPT or human claim as evidence
why: intent and explanation are editable assertions rather than reproduced facts
instead: validate and bind claims, preserve artifacts and check outputs, then compute a read-only verdict

pattern: letting wall-clock reporter fields or writable host paths enter a deterministic required-check boundary
why: elapsed-time noise breaks local-to-CI GateInput parity, while a deny-network-only sandbox still lets accepted checks mutate the source repository or runner files
instead: normalize reporter timing before hashing, scrub the check environment, mount the host read-only and expose only a per-run scratch directory

pattern: making developers and reviewers operate internal evidence identities, hashes and predicate syntax as the normal product workflow
why: proof machinery becomes ritual, obscures unexpected AI reach and failed promises, and makes a trustworthy decision harder rather than easier
instead: derive Intent, Reach and Readiness from exact accepted artifacts, surface exceptions and actions first, and keep raw contracts in secondary technical details
